economic_notes: TCN has investments in multiple limited partnerships and corporations including Cree Nation Partners LP #1, #2, #3, Iron North Limited Partnership (construction/contracting), Split Lake Security Services LP, 336/344 Thompson Drive LP (real estate). Portfolio investments include Keewatin Railway Company Ltd (44%), Ininew Project Management Ltd (38%), Aboriginal Strategies LP. Manitoba Hydro Adverse Effects and Future Development funding ongoing. Gull Rapids equity interest anticipated.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (year ended March 31, 2020), not 2024. Audit is QUALIFIED on three bases: (1) inability to audit Cree Nation Partners LP #1, #2, #3 (carried at $2,631,579, equity earnings $927,247); (2) inability to audit Iron North Limited Partnership consolidated figures (financial assets $3,482,826, TCA $3,971,186, liabilities $2,666,126, revenue $9,358,838, expenses $7,711,258); (3) $513,106 of destitute and bereavement payments recorded in VLT expense line without adequate documentation. Auditor firm name not clearly identified — report signed 'Chartered Professional Accountants' from Winnipeg, November 15, 2021. Net debt of $27,782,599. TCA closing net book value calculated from schedule: Buildings $43,774,030 + Equipment $4,686,550 + Vehicles $1,815,371 + Plane $380,529 + Infrastructure in-progress $38,231,442 + Construction in-progress $887,773 = approximately $89,851,545 (sum from net book value closing row). Housing Authority expenses ($515,733 CMHC + $3,458,811 Housing Authority = $3,974,544) combined for housing. annual_debt_service represents long-term debt repayments $1,172,074 + capital lease repayments $154,652 = $1,326,726. total_government_transfers = ISC $27,000,392 + Health Canada $4,699,817 + Province of Manitoba $658,007 + CMHC $461,919 + Manitoba Keewatinowi Okimakanak $993,071 + Other Government Organizations $8,852,105 (portion) — computed as total revenue minus OSR items (VLT $1,758,443 + Rental $815,227 + Gas Bar $803,144 + Smoke Shop $207,028 + Other $1,314,617 + Equity earnings $1,262,095 + Manitoba Hydro $6,108,966) = OSR ~$12,269,520, government transfers ~$42,665,311; figures approximate due to mixed classification of Manitoba Hydro and OGO.
